text.title: TDD with Red and Green in Separate Agents Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the test-tdd-red-green-split-agents methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: RED tool set MUST include Write(tests/) + Read(src/) and exclude Edit/Write(src/). GREEN tool set MUST include Edit/Write(src/) + Read(tests/) and exclude Write(tests/). Overlap is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Overlap = the agent that owns both can cheat by editing the test. Disjoint sets remove that lever entirely.

rule.statement: RED's prompt MUST be limited to the acceptance criterion (one sentence) + the source file headers it can read. Implementation hints are forbidden.
rule.rationale: If RED sees impl hints, it writes tests that match the hints, not the spec. The spec is the source of truth.

rule.statement: GREEN's prompt MUST be limited to the failing test diff and the runner output. The acceptance criterion text is not passed to GREEN.
rule.rationale: If GREEN sees the spec, it can paraphrase the spec into code that matches without making the test pass for the right reason.

rule.statement: The orchestrator MUST observe an actual `pytest -q` (or framework equivalent) RED state before invoking GREEN, and a GREEN state after. Skipping either observation is forbidden.

rationale.source: TDD literature (Beck, Kent) + Internal — orchestrator policy

rule.rationale: Without explicit RED/GREEN observations, the loop degenerates into `agent writes code + tests pass somehow`.

rule.statement: Merge to the integration branch is allowed only on observed GREEN with the RED-then-GREEN transition recorded in CI logs. Direct merges that skip the loop are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without an audit-trail gate, the split-agent ceremony is theatre — the project gains nothing over single-agent TDD.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
